questions.question: The term ‘accounting period’ is used to refer to the

options.A: time span during which taxes are paid to the Inland Revenue Board
options.B: Budget period, usually one year, relied on by the accountant
options.C: time span, usually one year covered by financial statement
options.D: period within which debtors are expected to settle accounts.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.ai_explanation: The accounting period refers to the time span, usually one year, covered by financial statements during which financial performance is measured and reported.

questions.question: Which of the following factors’ has aided the develop- ment of Accounting?

options.B: The discovery of mineral resources in commercial quantity.
options.C: The growth in size of businesses and the separation of ownership and management.
options.D: The development and management of a sophisticated monetary system.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.ai_explanation: The growth in size of businesses and the separation of ownership from management necessitated the development of formal accounting systems to track and report financial activities.

questions.question: Assigning revenues to the accounting period in which goods were sold or services rendered and expenses in- curred is known as
questions.subject: accounts
questions.ai_explanation: The matching concept requires that revenues be matched with the expenses incurred in earning them within the same accounting period.

questions.question: Which of the following concepts stipulates that account- ing profit is the difference between revenue and expenses?
questions.subject: accounts
questions.ai_explanation: The accrual concept stipulates that accounting profit is the difference between revenue earned and expenses incurred during a period, regardless of cash flows.

questions.question: The accounting convention which states that ‘profit must not be recognized until realized while all losses should be adequately provided for’ is termed
questions.subject: accounts
questions.ai_explanation: The conservatism (or prudence) convention states that profits should not be recognized until realized, while all potential losses should be adequately provided for.

questions.question: An effective accounting system should provide infor- mation

options.C: on internal and external reporting for managers and third parties
options.D: for promoters, directors, labour unions and distributors.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.ai_explanation: An effective accounting system should provide information for both internal reporting (for managers) and external reporting (for third parties like investors and creditors).

questions.question: Accounting information is used by investors and credi- tors of a company to predict

options.A: future cash flows of the company
options.B: future tax payments of the company
options.C: potential merger candidates for the company
options.D: appropriate remuneration for the company’s staff.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.ai_explanation: Investors and creditors use accounting information to assess and predict the company's future cash flows, which determine its ability to generate returns and repay obligations.

questions.question: Antics Electronics Company recently bought six genera- tors. Which of the following is the correct method of recording this transaction. A.Debit generator account and credit cash account.

options.B: Debit purchases account and credit cash account.
options.C: Debit cash account and credit purchases account.
options.D: Debit cash account and credit generator account.

questions.subject: accounts

questions.question: When a business incurs labours cost in installing a fixed asset, the cost is treated as
questions.subject: accounts
questions.ai_explanation: Labour costs incurred in installing a fixed asset are capitalized as an additional cost of the asset since they are necessary to make the asset ready for use.

questions.question: What is the cardinal rule of the double entry system?

options.A: Debit the increasing account and credit the decreasing account.
options.B: Debit the receiving account and credit the giving account.
options.C: Debit the asset account and credit the liability account.
options.D: Debit the revenue account and credit the expenditure account.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.ai_explanation: The cardinal rule of double entry bookkeeping is to debit the receiving account and credit the giving account for every transaction.

questions.question: Malam Gambo bought a freezer for his shop costing N 10,500. In recording, he debited office expenses account and credited the bank account. What book keeping error has he committed?
questions.subject: accounts
questions.ai_explanation: This is an error of principle because a fixed asset (freezer) was incorrectly recorded as an expense (office expenses) instead of being capitalized as an asset.

questions.question: The use of the folio in the ledger is for
questions.subject: accounts
questions.ai_explanation: The folio column in the ledger is used for referencing purposes, allowing cross-referencing between the journal and ledger entries.

questions.question: Account of Tanko Nig Ltd. Dr. Cr. N N 1/10 Bal. b/f 5,000 2/10 Cash 3,000 Sales 10,000 15/10 Bank 6,500 What is the closing balance of the account shown above?
questions.subject: accounts
questions.ai_explanation: Opening balance N5,000 (Dr) + Cash N3,000 (Dr) + Bank N6,500 (Cr) + Sales N10,000 (Cr) = Closing balance N5,500 (Cr).

questions.question: The suspense account is used to correct book keeping errors where the

options.D: error does not affect the agreement of the trial balance.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.ai_explanation: A suspense account is used when the item involved is unknown, allowing the trial balance to agree while the error is investigated.

questions.question: The discount column of a three column cashbook is not balanced off but periodically transferred to the discount accounts because discounts.

options.A: are not important in cash book
options.C: do not form part of double entry

questions.subject: accounts
questions.ai_explanation: Discount columns are not balanced off but transferred periodically to discount accounts because discounts do not form part of the double entry bookkeeping process in the cashbook.

questions.question: When is a petty cash account debited?

options.A: When the fund is established and every time money is spent
options.B: when the fund is established and every time it is replenished
options.C: when the fund is established and when the size of the float is decreased
options.D: Every time money is drawn from the petty cash.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.ai_explanation: A petty cash account is debited when the fund is first established and every time it is replenished with new cash.

questions.question: When preparing a bank reconciliation statement, which of the following is deducted from the balance per bank statement?
questions.subject: accounts
questions.ai_explanation: Unpresented cheques (cheques issued but not yet presented to the bank) are deducted from the bank statement balance during reconciliation.

questions.question: The financial position of an organization at a particular time can be ascertained from the

options.A: statement of sources and application of funds

questions.subject: accounts
questions.ai_explanation: The balance sheet shows the financial position of an organization at a particular point in time by listing assets, liabilities, and equity.

questions.question: Given: N Purchase 20,000 Sales 40,000 Carriage inwards 5,000 Carriage outwards 5,000 Opening stock 10,000 Closing stock 5,000 What is the cost of good sold?

questions.ai_explanation: Cost of goods sold = Opening stock (N10,000) + Purchases (N20,000) + Carriage inwards (N5,000) - Closing stock (N5,000) = N30,000.

questions.question: The net profit or loss for the year is determined in the profit and loss account after

questions.ai_explanation: Net profit or loss is determined after eliminating all expenses from gross profit and adding any other income such as commissions received.

options.A: income in the profit and loss account
options.C: addition cash in the profit and loss account

questions.subject: accounts
questions.ai_explanation: Recovery of a previously written-off bad debt is recorded as income in the profit and loss account as it represents a gain or recovery of funds.

questions.question: Beginning and ending accounts receivable balances were N 8,000 and N 15,000 respectively. If collections from customers during the period were N 36,000, them total sales on account would be
questions.subject: accounts

questions.question: Using the FIFO method of stock valuation, the ending inventory cost is
questions.subject: accounts
questions.ai_explanation: Using FIFO (First-In-First-Out) method, the ending inventory consists of the most recently purchased units; the calculation yields N 186 as the ending inventory cost.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.ai_explanation: Using LIFO (Last-In-First-Out) method, the ending inventory consists of the earliest purchased units; this method results in an ending inventory cost of N 196.

questions.question: The major difference between the receipt and payment account and the income and expenditure ac- count is that while the former

options.A: is kept by the treasurer, the latter is not
options.B: deals with all receipt and payments in the year regardless of the time it relates to the latter is for just that year
options.C: is a T-account the latter is not
options.D: is not in the ledger, the latter is. [PAGE 4]

questions.subject: accounts
questions.ai_explanation: The key difference is that the Receipt and Payment Account records all cash transactions regardless of when they relate to, while Income and Expenditure Account records transactions on an accrual basis for that specific period only.

questions.question: The following information relates to a hockey club: N Subscription owing at the beginning Subscription owing at the end Subscription received in the advance at the beginning Subscription received During the period 2,560 The amount to be shown in the income and expenditure account would be
questions.subject: accounts
questions.ai_explanation: Subscription income in the account = Beginning owing + Received during period - Ending owing - Beginning advance; calculation yields N 2,590 as the amount to be shown in the income and expenditure account.

questions.ai_explanation: Net profit from trading activities of a non-profit organization is treated as income and recorded in the income and expenditure account, which is the equivalent of a profit and loss account for non-profit entities.

questions.question: The major distinguishing element between the final ac- counts of a partnership and a sole trader is the
questions.subject: accounts
questions.ai_explanation: The appropriation account is the major distinguishing element in partnership final accounts as it shows how profits are distributed among partners, which is unique to partnerships and not found in sole trader accounts.

questions.question: Good will appears in the books of a business only if it has been

options.B: raised in connection with the admission of a new partner
options.C: raised to account for the true value of a business on the death of a partner
options.D: raised in order to prevent the balance sheet showing that the business is insolvent.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.ai_explanation: Goodwill appears in the books of a business only when it has been purchased at a certain price, as purchased goodwill is a tangible asset that must be recorded at its cost.

questions.question: Kayode, Akpan and Kachalla are in partnership. Their respective capital accounts had the following balances: N40,000 N50,000 and N70,000. The partners agree to ad- mit Wamo as a new partner with a one-fifth interest in the partnership capital in exchange for N50,000 cash. Wamo’s equity in the resulting partnership is
questions.subject: accounts
questions.ai_explanation: Total existing capital = N40,000 + N50,000 + N70,000 = N160,000; new total capital = N160,000 + N50,000 = N210,000; Wamo's one-fifth interest = N210,000 × 1/5 = N42,000.

questions.question: Under which of the following conditions is a partnership dissolved?

options.A: Change of the partnership’s head office.
options.C: Purchase of a large quatity of fixed assets.
options.D: Retirement of a manager who is not a partner.

questions.subject: accounts
questions.ai_explanation: A partnership is dissolved when a new partner is admitted because the existing partnership agreement is terminated and a new one must be formed with the additional partner.

questions.question: An advantage of using accounting ratio is that they
questions.subject: accounts
questions.ai_explanation: Accounting ratios facilitate decision-making by providing meaningful relationships between financial figures that help users analyze performance and make informed business decisions.

questions.question: The accounts of Buba (Nig.) Plc showed the following for the year ended December 31, 1993: N Gross sales 219,900 Sales returns and allowances 4,900 Net sales 215,000 Accounts receivable (net): 1/1/93 20,000 31/12/93 30,000 The turnover of accounts receivable is

questions.question: The expenditure on a good or service which is consumed either immediately or within a current accounting period is called
questions.subject: accounts
questions.ai_explanation: Recurrent expenditure refers to spending on goods or services that are consumed within the current accounting period, as opposed to capital expenditure which creates long-term assets.

questions.subject: accounts

questions.subject: accounts

questions.question: The amount for individual items on a government budget is called a

questions.subject: accounts
